video_title: The AI Crackdown Could Change the Internet Forever
gist: Bankless hosts NEAR co-founder Illia Polosukhin on the US export ban of Anthropic's Fable 5 and decentralized AI. He frames the ban as a dangerous precedent (any internet service can be geo-shut-down, balkanizing the net) that accelerates sovereign labs and open source — which he says reaches Fable-level within 3-6 months. Key market signals: AI cost pressure is real even in SV (Microsoft cancelled Claude after ~$1B; Uber capped usage), compute is the binding bottleneck (3-4 hyperscalers buying it all), agentic tools leak secrets, 'alignment is a meme,' and ad/revenue optimization is creeping into chatbots. The back half is a promotional pitch for NEAR's confidential-inference stack — weigh the bias.

insights.claim: Illia frames the US export ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 (citing jailbreak/safety) as a dangerous precedent: it shows any internet service — not just AI — can be geo-shut-down, balkanizing the borderless internet and putting every other government on alert.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: Undermines US-as-trusted-tech-exporter and accelerates sovereign AI labs + decentralized AI — a structural tailwind for the crypto-AI stack.
insights.quote: every single internet service can be shut down

insights.claim: Illia argues the ban backfires by spurring sovereign labs and decentralized AI worldwide, and predicts Fable-level capability will be available open-source within 3-6 months (JLM52 already benchmarks ~Claude 4.7) — a '4-minute-mile' effect where one party's breakthrough is quickly matched with compute.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: Frontier moats are short-lived; open-weights commoditize capability within months, pressuring closed-model pricing power.
insights.quote: I think we will have this level of model from open source one way or another within next 3 to 6 months

insights.claim: Illia expects that if export controls stand, AI labs must KYC and continuously re-verify users, and sees full nationalization of AI labs as plausible (the labs themselves call AI 'as powerful as nuclear weapons') — turning a desktop coding agent into 'a government agent on your computer.'
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: A privacy/regulatory regime shift for AI access — data-capture and nationalization risk reshape the consumer-AI landscape.

insights.claim: Illia says the cost differential is real even in Silicon Valley — Uber limited Claude usage and Microsoft cancelled its Claude subscription after spending ~$1B — while open/aggregated models with smart routing deliver ~90% of frontier capability at a large discount; Anthropic's parabolic revenue mirrors customers' parabolic spend.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: Unsustainable inference spend is driving model-routing/aggregation — a margin and demand risk for premium closed models.
insights.quote: Microsoft cancelled their claw subscription, right? Because they spent like a billion dollars

insights.claim: Illia identifies compute as the binding bottleneck: three-to-four hyperscalers are buying out essentially all new compute for the next 1-2 years, leaving little to aggregate — driving experiments to pool idle compute (Apple devices, time-zone load-balancing) as a decentralized alternative.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: Compute scarcity is the real constraint — decentralized/idle-compute aggregation is the opening it creates.
insights.quote: the bottleneck right now is actually compute

insights.claim: Illia predicts decentralized/aggregated AI gets adopted first in the global south and outside Silicon Valley — mirroring stablecoins (used for payments in Africa before developed markets) — where cost matters most and governments are restrictive.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: Watch emerging markets as the leading edge of low-cost open-model adoption, as with stablecoin payments.

insights.claim: Illia warns agentic coding tools (e.g., Claude Cowork) accidentally read private keys, secrets, passwords and health records and ship them to provider logs — and confidentiality becomes more valuable as companies route decisions through AI, since training on that data lets a lab 'replicate your business.'
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: Enterprise IP/confidentiality risk is a real adoption barrier for agentic tools — and the demand driver for confidential inference.
insights.quote: this agents they accidentally read all your private keys and secrets and passwords and sends them to their entropic

insights.claim: Illia argues general AI 'alignment is a meme' — humans aren't aligned with each other, so you can only align a model to a single person or to a company (which optimizes for revenue) — and ties safety-team departures to this impossibility.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: A skeptical frame on 'aligned AI' marketing — alignment in practice serves the operator's incentives, usually revenue.
insights.quote: you can only do alignment with a single person

insights.claim: Drawing on his Google experience, Illia says engineers AB-test new systems for revenue, so products drift toward making money over user success (e.g., half-answers that drive more searches/ads) — citing OpenAI's foundation-to-for-profit shift, and arguing web3 token models align holders=users.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: Predicts ad-monetization and revenue-optimization creeping into chatbots — a quality/trust risk for centralized AI products.
insights.quote: you naturally as engineers start optimizing for what will produce more money for the company

insights.claim: Illia's core thesis: the more restrictive governments become, the more bullish decentralized AI/crypto — following the fintech/stablecoin arc — and labs may decentralize rather than be nationalized; he's building NEAR's confidential-inference stack (near.com, Ironclaw private agent, agent marketplace, confidential 'private shard') targeting SME/prosumer users in finance, legal and medical.
insights.speaker: Illia Polosukhin (NEAR)
insights.so_what: A named decentralized-AI/privacy vehicle positioned to benefit from AI restriction — note the heavy promotional bias and execution risk vs incumbents.
insights.quote: if they don't want to get nationalized they will become decentralized
